name: Sasol Ltd.

profile.description: Sasol is an integrated energy and chemicals company headquartered in Sandton, South Africa. It operates coal-to-liquids and gas-to-liquids synfuels facilities at Secunda and Mozambique, and produces specialty chemicals globally. It is Africa's largest private-sector GHG emitter.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Proprietary Fischer-Tropsch synfuels technology is historically significant but hasn't been materially extended in the last 5 years. [1] 'Future Sasol' strategy aims for net-zero by 2050 with a 30% GHG reduction by 2030 - genuine but mostly through efficiency gains (~5% reduction achieved by 2023). [3] No AI deployment evidence. Meaningful regional market share in South Africa fuels but not a defining global category leader. Pipeline visibility limited to operational recovery and sustainable aviation fuel aspirations. R&D modest. Score: track record 1 + AI 0 + share 1 + pipeline 1 + R&D 0 = 3.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Purpose 'Innovating for a better world' with triple-bottom-line of People, Planet, Profit is specific and thoughtful (3 points). [3] As a major South African employer, Sasol has long-standing community investment including education and local economic development programs (1 point). No retention data surfaced; CEO Simon Baloyi is a 22-year internal Sasol veteran appointed April 2024 which is a positive cultural signal but insider buying is neutral. Score: 3 + 1 + 0 + 0 = 4.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Weak quantitative profile: ROE only 2.5% (below 15%), profit margin 0.97% (well below 20%), D/E 70% (at target), Revenue growth YoY 0.2% (below 10%), 5yr CAGR near zero. Forward P/E 5.75 is attractive (target <20). Trailing P/E 56 reflects depressed earnings (H1 FY2026 EPS -89% to -99% YoY per company guidance). [1] Liquidity passes ($12.98 x 2.82M = $36.6M).

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: Sasol's core business - coal-to-liquids and gas-to-liquids fuel production, petrochemicals, and coal mining - is physical-industrial infrastructure that AI cannot displace. [2] AI may improve operational reliability at Secunda but analysts are focused on commodity prices, currency, and decarbonization, not AI substitution risk. No meaningful AI revenue. Score: protection 8 + opportunity 0 = 8.

infinite_game.summary: Sasol articulates a long-horizon Future Sasol decarbonization strategy but execution is hampered by balance sheet stress and macro headwinds; mixed signals overall.

just_cause.analysis: 'Innovating for a better world' with triple-bottom-line framing is a genuine cause; 2050 net zero ambition has resource commitment behind it, though credibility is contested given coal-based feedstock legacy. [3] 1 point.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Committing to 30% GHG reduction by 2030 from a coal-to-liquids base is a costly commitment; however, repeated guidance cuts suggest short-term firefighting dominates. [1] 1 point.

trusting_teams.analysis: CEO Simon Baloyi is a 22-year internal veteran appointed April 2024 - continuity signal. [2] Large unionized workforce with labor risks typical of SA mining/industrial. 1 point.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Sasol has evolved from purely synfuels to a global chemicals business and is now attempting a decarbonization pivot. [3] Incremental, not transformative. 1 point.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Competitive commentary is supply/demand focused, no evidence of learning-oriented rival framing. 0 points.

investment_thesis: Sasol trades at an optically cheap 5.75x forward P/E with a $5.75B+ market cap on $13.9B TTM revenue, offering deep-value exposure to South African energy, Secunda synfuels, and global specialty chemicals. [1] CEO Simon Baloyi's April 2024 appointment (22-year internal tenure) brings operational continuity and a Capital Markets Day roadmap for Future Sasol decarbonization and energy system contribution. Secunda production improved 9% YoY in the most recent quarter on better coal quality.
thesis_continued: Bear case dominates: H1 FY2026 EPS is guided at R0.10-R0.80 versus R7.22 prior year - an 89%-99% decline driven by oil prices and ZAR currency headwinds. [1] Adjusted EBITDA is guided at R19-23B vs R24B prior. ROE of 2.5% and profit margin of ~1% mean Sasol is barely earning its cost of capital. D/E of 70% sits at the IOWN target but remains high absolute leverage for a commodity-cyclical business. The 2050 net-zero pivot from a coal-based feedstock base is capital intensive and could strand legacy assets.
key_catalysts: Oil price recovery and ZAR stabilization lifting H2 FY2026 EPS back toward normalized ~R14 levels
Secunda operational improvements continuing (+9% YoY production) and delivering unit cost improvements
Future Sasol Capital Markets Day execution and Scope 1/2 GHG reduction milestones
key_risks: Further EPS downside from oil price decline or ZAR weakness - already guided down 89-99% YoY
South African Eskom electricity, logistics (Transnet rail), and regulatory risk compressing margins
Stranded-asset risk as 2050 net-zero pivot accelerates while legacy coal-to-liquids remains the revenue base
sources: Sasol H1 FY2026 results and guidance, Moneyweb, 2026
Sasol ESG Climate Action page, sasol.com/esg, accessed April 2026
